The LCE advised the City Treasurer to comply with the recommendation to coordinate with the transfer of funds from PNB and LBP, and inform the City Administrator of such transfer.

observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant already prepared the necessary adjusting entries to reflect reclassifications of previous acquisitions inadvertently recorded erroneously. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
LCE require the
(a) head of the CUPDO as the lead office to oversee/monitor/consolidate the progress of implementation of PPAs included in the AIP; (b) head of CENRO to closely coordinate with CUPDO and submit the accomplishment report on PPAs under the 20% DF for consolidation of CUPDO; and (c) heads of departments with PPAs funded by 20% DF to coordinate with the CUPDO for proper monitoring of implementation; and (d) General Services Office and Bids and Awards Committee to plan and implement in a timely fashion the procurement process so as to deliver the PPAs within the timeframe indicated in the AIP.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE directed the CUPDO head to comply with the audit recommendation. CUPDO, and in order to ensure proper monitoring of progress of implementation, came up with an internal reporting template entitled, “Report Utilization of 20% Development Fund” since the first semester of 2019.
As the lead office, CUPDO required the offices with projects funded under 20% DF to submit the required accomplishment report and the offices with projects funded under 20% DF undertook to coordinate with the CUPDO for proper monitoring and implementation and with GSO, and BAC with respect to the procurement process for the implementation of PPAs within the timeframe indicated in the AIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
LCE require
(a) the City Engineering Office, budget and accounting offices to properly plan programs and projects, to use the proper and appropriate budgetary and financial accounts and the correct project name or title of programs and projects for a more reliable scope, information and reporting in the financial statements to avoid unrelated and improper charges to the LDRRMF; and (b) the CDRRMO to ensure that programmed projects in the AIP for LDRRMF are strictly implemented within the project’s specified contract time; (c) the City Engineer to compute for the liquidated damages (LD) for every day of delay; (d) the City Accountant to obtain from the City CEO the computed amount of LD for proper action in the next billing of the contractor; (e) the City Engineer to monitor the execution/implementation of every line item included in the project cost such as the safety and health and cause the refund of the cost of safety and health from the Contractor; and (f) the City Engineer to inquire on the health status of the injured laborer for medical assistance and conduct further investigation on the accident for immediate proper action.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE already required the City Engineer, City Budget Officer and the CAO to closely coordinate with each other with regard to the proper titling of government projects relative to the project scope, the proper charging of the appropriate budgetary and financial account, the proper charging of any and all charges needing to be deducted from the payments to be made to the contractor.
The CDRRMO Head undertook to properly monitor the implementation of projects funded under LDRRMF within the project’s contract time.
As to the computation of liquidated damages, the contractor, AZUL Builders, was granted a 45-day contract extension through a letter dated January 14, 2020, which made March 06, 2020 as the new date of contract completion. The CEO conducted an inspection on March 05, 2020.
Further, the CEO conducted project site inspections on all projects at least once a week to identify delays and conflicts on the construction site. During said site visits, inspectors also reiterated that mandatory safety measures for the construction workers should be practiced. The CEO strictly imposed the observance of Construction Occupational Safety and Health (COSH) on all projects.
Moreover, the CEO properly monitored the implementation of every line item included in every project cost.
The injured laborer already recovered.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
LCE require the
(a) CEO to conduct strict
supervision on the on-going
projects for the timely
completion of projects in
accordance with the
timeframe in the AIP; and (b) CDRRMO to closely
monitor/ oversee all projects
funded by the LDRRMF to
achieve a 100%
implementation rate using
the fund for disaster related
projects.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE required the City Engineer to conduct strict supervision on the on-going projects of the city.
Moreover, the LCE already ordered the CDRRMO to closely monitor the implementation of the projects funded by the LDRRMF.
The concerned offices undertook to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
LCE require the City
Treasurer
(a) change the combination
lock of the Main Vault and

observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer already changed the combination lock of the main vault where such combination is personal to her.
According to the City Treasurer, the unused official receipts (ORs) stacked in her office were the newly requisitioned ORs from the National Printing Office to be numbered. After doing such, said ORs will be transferred to the main vault.
For proper storage, they were advised to request for a suitable cabinet for the office supplies and for a new vault to be used by the disbursing officer. On the other hand, they undertook to make the necessary request with the GSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
LCE (a) direct strict compliance with the rules and procedures governing the operation of the RGPs such as (i) issuance of a departure ticket to vehicle drivers for every trip made and copy of departure ticket. (ii) wearing of Identification Cards (ID) to establish the proper identity of the dispatchers; and (iii) forging of a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the City Government of Meycauayan and the UV Express, jeepneys’ and tricycles group using the CTT for the assignment of bays and as basis for paying the corresponding fees to CEEMO; and (b) direct the CEEMO Head to stop performing collection functions and the City Treasurer’s Office to perform its mandated functions of taking charge of the collections of receipts and revenues of the three RGPs pursuant to Section 170 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 366 and 4 of City Ordinances 219 and 221.
observations.managementAction:
(a) (i) As per CEEMO head, official receipts (ORs) were issued for payments of terminal fee on every departure made instead of departure ticket. (ii) Identification cards were issued by transport groups to their respective dispatchers, in addition to them being required to wear proper uniforms. (iii) Transport groups occupying the Meycauayan Common Terminal used a uniform set of fees as found under the New Revenue Code. As for the drafting of the recommended Memorandum of Agreement (MOA), the city drafted the said agreement with secured terms and conditions.
(b) Since the Office of the City Treasurer lacked personnel, CEEMO-cash bonded employees were assigned to do the collection as monitored by the City Treasurer. However, the LCE will require the concerned offices to comply with City governing rules and regulations on the subject.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
LCE require the
(a) GAD Focal Person to: (i) implement GAD projects according to the GPB; (ii) set-up/validate and maintain an updated GAD database which will serve as basis for planning, formulating policies, identifying, monitoring and evaluating GAD responsive projects, programs and activities; (iii) establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system to provide the details of accomplishment reports together with the supporting documents for proper validation of expenses attributable to the GAD fund; and (iv) coordinate with all offices concerned with regard to the utilization of the GAD fund; and (b) City Accounting Office to continue the preparation of subsidiary ledgers for the utilization of the GAD Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD database was already set-up, as well as a subsidiary ledger for GAD-related activities was maintained by assigned personnel. The GAD Focal Person, however, was advised to identify all proposed projects of different departments to be included in the annual GAD Plan by making the necessary communication with the concerned department heads.
The subsidiary ledgers for the utilization of the GAD Fund were continuously being updated as transactions to the Fund were received and processed in the Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
LCE through the CHRMO:
(a) require the JOs and CoS personnel to submit their jobdescription, duties and project assignment duly aligned with the function of their respective offices, signed by the Head of Offices and duly approved by the Local Chief Executive; (b) ensure close monitoring of their accomplishments based on the expected outputs in their job description; and (c) consider reducing the number of JOs and CoS by objectively assessing the need of each office vis-a-vis the performance of JOs and CoS in order to effectively and efficiently achieve the LGU’s mandate and their respective function.
observations.managementAction:
The CHRMO already issued a memorandum requiring all JO’s and COS, through their department heads, to submit their job description with an instruction to the department heads to ensure that duties and assignments of their JOs and COS were aligned with the function of their respective offices, to which the offices already complied.
Also, the CHRMO undertook to closely monitor the actual duties of the JOs and COS to make sure that their accomplishments were based on their job descriptions. For their part, the CHRMO instructed her staff to thoroughly review all of the accomplishment reports and to coordinate with the head of office for discrepancies.
Reduction on the number of JOs and COS depended primarily on the performance evaluation and recommendation of their respective department heads. Nevertheless, the CHRMO was directed to observe the audit recommendation on the JOs and COS.

No.1445; and (c) City Human Resource Management Office to study the present manning at the CAO and CTO, if warranted and if the budget permits, employ additional personnel for these offices to carry-out their functions effectively.
observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer was advised to observe the timely submission of RCI, all paid vouchers, payrolls, and other financial documents and reports to the Accounting Office.
The LCE already advised the City Accountant to comply with the audit recommendation.
The CHRMO was directed to study the staffing pattern of the City Accountant’s and City Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
LCE require the head of the
CENRO to
(a) install effective control
measures to properly monitor
compliance of barangays with
R.A. No. 9003 particularly
the establishment of MRF and
segregation of solid wastes at
source to minimize cost of
tipping fees; (b) strictly monitor the departure time of hauler’s trucks from the MRF and reconcile the discrepancy in the list of accredited trucks or authorized vehicles to haul garbage from the City’s MRF to its final disposal facility; (c) avoid manual sorting of wastes by the JO personnel and utilize all machines and equipment at the MRF to the maximum; and (d) draft a proposal for approval of the agency head considering the use of the City’s own garbage trucks for the hauling and final disposal to ISWMS to save on hauling cost.
observations.managementAction:
CENRO, through the barangay Solid Waste Management Council (SWMC), already implemented the “Yes to Collection, Yes to Segregation!” Campaign which was conducted in twenty-six (26) barangays on the last quarter of 2019. During the series of the said information campaign, the provisions of RA No. 9003 (Ecological Solid Waste Management act of 2000) was explained and emphasized. During the regular quarterly meeting of SWMC, the status of MRFs were checked by the LCE.
CENRO is currently maintaining a logbook to monitor the hauler’s trucks from the MRF. However, management will heed the recommendations to fully strengthen the monitoring process of the hauler’s trucks in order to reconcile any discrepancies, if any.
As much as management wants to heed the recommendation, the City’s central MRF at Brgy. Pajo is designed for manual sorting only, thus, second man handling is quite essential for the segregation.
The LCE already advised CENRO to study and draft a proposal regarding the use of the City’s own garbage trucks for the hauling and final disposal to ISWMS to save on hauling cost. CENRO, on the other hand, undertook to make a study on the necessary proposal.

We recommended that Management (a) require the officials concerned to optimize  the utilization of DF for PPA under its ADP ; (b) expedite the implementation of all developmental projects  identified in the  Annual Investment Program for the  timely completion of all  the   programmed socio- economic development and environmental projects  of the City  pursuant to JMC No. 2017 -1 dated February 22, 2017;(c) take immediate priority action on the PPAs needing realignment to avoid delays in the implementation of PPAs programmed for future undertakings; (d) determine the cost of actual accomplishments of  all the unfinished projects and compute for the liquidated damages to be imposed to  the contractor equal to at least one tenth (1/10) of the cost of the  unperformed  portion of the works  for every day of delay;  and (e) require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to   furnish  invitation letters to at least two additional observers aside from COA ,  strictly comply with the requirement of invitation in all stages of the procurement  and  post the bidding documents in the agency’s website ; and (f) submit the lacking documentary requirements  to the COA Auditor such as the  ABC, Original and As-Built Plans, SWA, POW and Detailed Estimate with Unit Cost Derivation.

We recommended that Management: (a) request for assistance from DOH on the operation and management  of a tertiary hospital through the City Health Officer and  rescind the contract of lease with WWMFI which defeated the very purpose of putting up the facility for  indigent constituents; (b) require the GSO to properly account for all the property and equipment owned by the City and ensure that Property Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment   is issued to end-users for proper accountability; and (c) require the contractors to (i) finish the elevator works and restore the demolished portions into a suitable entry point for huge machines; and  (ii)  refund the cost of the  undelivered 25 hospital beds,   the overpriced amount for the delivered hospital beds of ₱7,025,000.00 and the overstatement in  project cost of ₱32,686,341.40 due to inclusion of non-infrastructure items, equipment and supplies.

We recommended that the City Accountant monitor the immediate transfer of the LDRRMF unutilized balance to the appropriate special trust fund account and effect the preparation and maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the special trust fund account.
We recommended that Management prioritize the preparation of the CLUP and ZO and ensure that the CCAP is integrated thereto, submitted for review and approval of the Sanggunian on a timely basis.

We recommended that the (a) CSWDO conduct actual validation of the  families and individuals in the affected barangays to come up with the victim profiles/database; (b) submit to CDRRMO the provision for data base generation of disaster victims, relief goods  and temporary shelter for inclusion in the AIP for DRRMF;  (c) CDRRMO prepare the LDRRMP and  involve the CSWDO and other concerned offices in the AIP  deliberation to include database generation, stockpile of relief goods,  provision of temporary shelter  and ensure appropriateness of procurement for disaster need; and (d) City Engineering Office and  in joint coordination with the Bureau of Fire Protection, conduct fire prevention inspection of business establishments,  residential areas and other public places.

We recommended that the City Mayor  a) direct the CDRRMO to monitor closely the implementation of projects, programs, and activities in the AIP; b) instruct the City Budget Officer and  City Accountant to desist from improperly charging non-related disaster expenses against the DRRMF; and c) require the BAC Chairman to coordinate with the GSO in the procurement activities to comply with the process of public bidding.

We recommended that the Local School Board (a) strictly adhere to the provisions of JC No. 1 s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG on the proper utilization of the SEF for its intended purpose only; and (b) stop the practice of granting cash advances to DepEd officials/employees using the SEF and instead, consider making payment of SEF expenditures directly to service providers.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the: (a) Chief of the CPDO to cause the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database which will serve as basis for planning, formulating GAD responsive projects, programs and activities; and  (b) establish and submit the details of accomplishment reports together with its supporting documents to validate the expenses charged to GAD fund.

We recommended that Management require the (a) BAC and GSO to ensure that procured medicines do exceed the one quarter requirement; (b)  CHO and CHUs to conduct regular inventory of medicines taking into consideration the required minimum shelf life and  to properly keep, store, and account for the medicines by assigning permanent personnel to handle the storage rooms for proper accountability; (c) City Accounting Office to recognize the procurement of drugs and medicines as  inventories;   (d) GSO to impose penalty for late delivery of medicines  under P.O. No. 2017-12-017 dated December 15, 2017 issued to  Synapse Trading amounting to ₱15,764,374.50; and (e) the requisitioning officer attach the  certification that the medicines being  procured fall within and conform with the latest edition of the PNF Manual.

We  recommended that  the City Mayor: (a) hold liable the City Engineering Officials and other concerned City officials and the contractor  for the excessive  contract cost arising from  non-compliance with the DPWH standard on Overhead, Contingencies and Miscellaneous (OCM) of 15% of EDC for contracts with EDC of Above P50M up to P150M; (b) submit   explanation for the failure to create a Design and Build Committee  and submit the Preliminary Design and Construction Studies  for the Design and Build Contract ; and  (c) require the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to  explain  the discrepancies   between the advertised and approved ABC and other auditorial and technical deficiencies observed in the bidding process.
We recommended that Management require the CENRO to prioritize the conduct of waste characterization studies for integration to the SWMP from CY 2017 to   2026.

We recommended that Management require the 16 barangays without MRF to locate a suitable space for establishing their MRF so the residual wastes will be minimized hence, reduce the final disposal fee being paid by the City.

We recommended that Management require the CENRO to provide the waste compartment of all garbage trucks with cover to ensure the containment of the solid wastes while in transit and inform all personnel assigned on the roads to monitor violations of the prohibited acts which include the open burning of solid wastes and apply the corresponding penalty.

We recommended that Management (a) require the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to submit the financial reports and documents within the first ten days of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; and (b) consider augmenting the personnel complement in the Accounting Office in order to comply with the submission of accounts requirement within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management (a) direct the CEEMO to coordinate with the developer of the public market for the submission of legal documents to document  any change in the  ownership  of the latter in order that the City may be properly guided in transacting with them; (b)   require the developer to inform the City of any adjustments made in the rentals and charges to the tenants of the market; (c) direct the officials concerned to be diligent on the civil case filed for the expropriation of lot in the transport terminal in order to protect the City’s interest and avoid the risk of losing the City’s investment in the development of the terminal; and  (d) execute lease agreements/rental contracts with the concerned stallholders of the canteen to enforce obligations of both parties.

We recommended that the City Mayor through the HRMO: a) hire only those personnel actually needed by respective offices and conduct strict monitoring of  attendance and submission of accomplishment reports; and (b) hold responsible heads of offices on the noted lapses of JOs and the recovery of payments made for services not actually rendered to the City.

We recommended that the City Accountant (a) strictly follow BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof and remit in full the taxes actually collected by the City within the prescribed period to avoid any penalty; and (b) require the concerned personnel to exert more efforts in the verification of the unremitted amount and remit in full the amount due to BIR.

We recommended that the City Accountant a) require the concerned personnel to maintain subsidiary records of all GSIS members of the City for proper monitoring of accounts and b) verify further those accounts with over or under deduction or remittance and make the necessary adjustment.

We recommended that the City Accountant a) require the concerned personnel to maintain subsidiary records of all Philhealth members of the City for proper monitoring of accounts and b) verify further those accounts with over or under deduction or remittance and make the necessary adjustment.

We recommended that the City Accountant (a) require the concerned personnel to maintain subsidiary records of all Pag-Ibig members of the City for proper monitoring of accounts; and (b) verify further those accounts with over or under deduction or remittance and make the necessary adjustment.

We recommended that Management immediately require the settlement of said disallowances by complying with the requirements stated thereon pursuant to the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
